trial_label: Breast Cancer Study of Preoperative Pembrolizumab + Radiatio

rubric.target_patient: Women 18+ with newly diagnosed, non-metastatic, ER-positive/HER2-negative invasive breast adenocarcinoma who are high-risk by histologic grade II-III, Ki-67>20%, and/or ER expression <75% by IHC, planned for breast-conserving surgery and/or neoadjuvant systemic therapy, with no prior checkpoint inhibitor or other immune-checkpoint agent exposure and no subglandular breast implants with altered tissue. This is a preoperative window-of-opportunity population identified at the point of diagnosis, before definitive local therapy, within a multidisciplinary breast oncology practice.
rubric.site_burden: This is a single-site, investigator-initiated Phase 1/2 study requiring tight coordination between medical oncology, radiation oncology, breast surgery, and a research infrastructure capable of serial tissue and blood collection. Per the schedule of assessments, patients receive two IV pembrolizumab doses 3 weeks apart, with an RT boost (8 Gy x 3 fractions) delivered concurrently with the second dose within 5 business days of C2D1, followed by initiation of standard-of-care surgery or systemic therapy ~3 weeks later. The protocol mandates serial mandatory research tumor biopsies (baseline, pre-C2D1, and pre-Week 7/SOC initiation), repeated research blood draws (minimum 3 CPT + 1 red top + 1 green top tubes at each timepoint), extensive endocrine/safety labs, EKG, cosmesis evaluations with digital photography, and optional MRI and fecal microbiome sub-studies. Follow-up continues at minimum every 3 weeks through week 19, then every 6 months for 1 year, with irAE monitoring extending to 1 year post-treatment. This demands an integrated breast cancer program with same-site radiation oncology, image-guided/core biopsy capability on a tight research schedule, a research pharmacy able to handle pembrolizumab under an IIT IND, and a clinical research team experienced in immunotherapy toxicity monitoring and multi-specimen biobanking logistics.

criteria.requirement: The investigator must be a breast medical oncologist, breast surgical oncologist, or radiation oncologist with a dedicated breast cancer practice, since this trial sequences pembrolizumab with a radiation boost prior to breast surgery/systemic therapy.
criteria.satisfies_if: NPPES taxonomy lists medical oncology, hematology-oncology, radiation oncology, or surgical oncology with a documented breast cancer subspecialty focus, or self-attestation confirms a breast-cancer-focused practice.
criteria.derived_from: Study design: preoperative pembrolizumab + RT boost prior to breast-conserving surgery/systemic therapy in breast cancer patients

criteria.requirement: The investigator should have prior clinical or research experience with anti-PD-1/PD-L1 checkpoint inhibitor therapy, given the need to manage and monitor immune-related adverse events through 1 year post-treatment.
criteria.satisfies_if: PubMed record includes at least one publication on checkpoint inhibitor therapy in breast or other solid tumors, or ClinicalTrials.gov lists the physician as an investigator on a prior anti-PD-1/PD-L1 trial.
criteria.derived_from: Intervention: pembrolizumab (anti-PD-1); irAE monitoring through 1 year post-treatment

criteria.requirement: Familiarity with tumor immune microenvironment biomarkers (e.g., tumor infiltrating lymphocytes) is valuable given the trial's key secondary endpoint of TIL score change.
criteria.satisfies_if: PubMed record includes at least one publication involving tumor infiltrating lymphocytes, immune biomarkers, or tumor immune microenvironment in breast cancer.
criteria.derived_from: Primary outcome: Changes in Tumor Infiltrating Lymphocyte Score (TILs)

criteria.requirement: The practice must regularly diagnose and manage ER-positive/HER2-negative breast cancer patients meeting high-risk criteria (grade II-III, Ki-67>20%, or ER<75%) at the point of initial diagnosis, prior to surgery.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms the practice evaluates and stages a meaningful volume (e.g., >=10/year) of newly diagnosed ER+/HER2- breast cancer patients meeting the trial's high-risk definition prior to definitive local therapy.
criteria.derived_from: Inclusion: High-risk, ER-positive and HER2-negative breast cancer defined by grade, Ki-67, and ER expression thresholds

criteria.requirement: The site must have an established clinical pathway for identifying and referring newly diagnosed, non-metastatic breast cancer patients into a preoperative window-of-opportunity protocol before surgery or neoadjuvant systemic therapy begins.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms the practice has a multidisciplinary tumor board or referral pathway that captures newly diagnosed breast cancer patients prior to initiation of surgery or neoadjuvant therapy.
criteria.derived_from: Trial design: two pembrolizumab doses and RT boost occur before planned breast-conserving surgery or neoadjuvant systemic therapy

criteria.requirement: The site must have radiation oncology facilities capable of delivering a coordinated RT boost (8 Gy x 3 fractions) within 5 business days of the second pembrolizumab dose.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report or NPPES confirms on-site or tightly affiliated radiation oncology department capable of scheduling fractionated boost RT on a protocol-mandated timeline concurrent with drug administration.
criteria.derived_from: Operational burden: RT boost (8Gy x 3 fractions) delivered concurrently with C2D1 within 5 business days

criteria.requirement: The site must be able to perform serial image-guided or core tumor biopsies at baseline, pre-C2D1, and pre-Week 7, coordinated with surgical/pathology teams and research biobanking.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms the site has interventional radiology or breast surgery capacity to perform mandatory research tumor biopsies at the three protocol-specified timepoints with fresh tissue processing available.
criteria.derived_from: Operational burden: Research tumor biopsy required at baseline, pre-C2D1, and pre-Week 7/SOC initiation, with fresh tissue requirement for TNBC cohort

criteria.requirement: The site must have research infrastructure for handling multiple concurrent biospecimen collections (blood, tumor tissue, optional fecal samples) at each visit without added venipunctures burden.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms a dedicated clinical research coordinator/lab team capable of processing CPT tubes, biopsy specimens, and optional stool/MRI sub-studies per protocol schedule.
criteria.derived_from: Operational burden: research bloods (CPT tubes), fecal microbiome samples, optional MRI collected at multiple visits

criteria.requirement: The investigator should have prior experience serving as an investigator on an investigator-initiated trial (IIT) or early-phase oncology study, reflecting the operational complexity of running this single-site IIT protocol.
criteria.satisfies_if: ClinicalTrials.gov lists the physician as principal investigator or sub-investigator on at least one prior Phase 1/2 investigator-initiated oncology trial.
criteria.derived_from: Sponsor: Stephen Shiao (OTHER); Phase 1/2 investigator-initiated trial (IIT2017-07-HO-PEMBRORT)

criteria.requirement: Documented research funding relationships with Merck (pembrolizumab manufacturer) or other immuno-oncology sponsors would support ability to procure and administer study drug under IIT sponsorship.
criteria.satisfies_if: Open Payments records show research payments from Merck or another checkpoint inhibitor manufacturer to the investigator or their institution.
criteria.derived_from: Intervention: Pembrolizumab (Merck product) administered under investigator-initiated IND

criteria.requirement: The investigator must demonstrate capability to coordinate a multidisciplinary team (medical oncology, radiation oncology, surgery, pathology) under GCP for a complex biomarker-driven protocol with irAE monitoring extending to 1 year.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms the investigator has led or co-led a multidisciplinary tumor board-integrated clinical trial with cross-specialty procedural coordination and long-term AE follow-up.
criteria.derived_from: Operational burden: multidisciplinary sequencing of drug, RT boost, surgery/systemic therapy with follow-up to 1 year

excluded_screening: ECOG performance status 0-1 — a per-patient functional status check, not a physician-fit indicator
Hematologic/renal/hepatic lab thresholds (WBC, ANC, platelets, hemoglobin, creatinine, AST/ALT, bilirubin, INR) — standard per-patient eligibility labs, unrelated to site capability
Negative HIV/Hepatitis B/C screening — per-patient infectious disease screening required before drug administration
Contraception requirements and pregnancy testing for WOCBP — per-patient reproductive safety screening
Not breastfeeding — per-patient exclusion unrelated to investigator qualifications
Informed consent capacity — per-patient consent process, not a site-selection criterion
Immunodeficiency/chronic steroid or immunosuppressive therapy exclusion — per-patient medical history exclusion
Live vaccine restriction within 30 days — per-patient timing exclusion
Prior systemic anti-cancer therapy or investigational agent washout windows — per-patient timing exclusion
Cosmetic subglandular breast implant exclusion — anatomic per-patient eligibility criterion, not a site capability issue
Absence of metastatic disease confirmation — per-patient staging requirement, not a physician-fit indicator
Prior anti-PD-1/PD-L1 or other checkpoint agent exposure exclusion — per-patient treatment history exclusion
